programme.title: Bass Pro Shops Fisherman's Handbook
programme.desc: Professional anglers travel the country fishing for a living. In doing so, they become masters of their craft. From January through December, they are constantly figuring out how to get bass to bite. Gerald Swindle and Jason Christie are featured in this show highlighting springtime fishing. Tune in as these top touring pros offer insight into how they target bass during the spring.

programme.title: Fishing University
programme.desc: Elite angler Rob Gee and BPT pro Nick Hatfield join the crew for an offshore beatdown on TN's Douglas lake.

programme.title: Lindner's Angling Edge
programme.desc: Al Lindner shows the power and efficiency of the swim jig for both largemouth and smallmouth bass.

programme.title: In Fisherman TV
programme.desc: It's wild fishing action in real time as the In-Fisherman staff explores predator-prey relationships, highlighting fishing for smallmouths targeting gobies on the Door County waters of Lake Michigan; focusing on the importance of shad on big rivers for blue catfish; knocking on the woody lairs where panfish hide to catch largemouth bass.

programme.title: Jon Thelen's Destination Fish
programme.desc: Jon drops into Missouri's Harry S. Truman Lake, targeting crappies stacked in flooded timber. He details how sonar and pinpoint jig placement turn a maze of branches into a crappie goldmine. Later, Jon heads north for early-summer jigging action - when light tackle, current edges, and subtle movement make all the difference.

programme.title: Bill Dance Saltwater
programme.desc: Florida's Space Coast is a great place to catch snook and Captain Peter Deeks knows just where to find them.

programme.title: Bill Dance Outdoors
programme.desc: Listen up as Bill explains just how significant and versatile the sense of hearing is to the bass.
